title: UROUTER_SHUT
text: UROUTER_SHUT Shut down the Uniface Router. operation UROUTER_SHUT params string Mode : IN ; (C1) string Message : IN ; (C80) endparams Parameters Mode is the stop-mode: F for forced or N for normal. In forced mode, all servers are stopped, clients are disconnected, and the Uniface Router exits. In normal mode, existing clients are allowed to deliver requests to servers until the server reports it has no state, that is, it has no locks and no context (active component instances). At that moment the server is stopped. Once all the servers are stopped, the remaining clients are disconnected and the Uniface Router exits. Message is a message that is displayed in all running monitors and is returned to new clients or monitors that are refused connections. Return Values If successful, $status is set to 0. Otherwise, see the "Return Codes" section in UROUTMON . Use Allowed in all Uniface component types. Description The privileged-user operation UROUTER_SHUT shuts down the Uniface Router in normal or forced mode. All new connection requests are refused and no new servers will be started. This operation returns once the request is posted at the Uniface Router. This operation might fail if the Uniface Router is already shutting down at an equal or higher mode. Was this topic helpful?
